definitions.Application Engineering: Roles that BUILD software products — web apps, mobile apps, APIs, microservices, embedded systems. Primary output is running software used by end users or other systems.
definitions.Cyber Security: Roles whose primary job is securing systems, networks, and applications. Pen testing, threat detection, GRC compliance, SOC operations, IAM, application security.
definitions.Data & Intelligence: Roles focused on data — building pipelines, warehouses, ML models, analytics platforms, BI reports, knowledge graphs, feature stores. Primary output is data products.
definitions.Infrastructure & Cloud: Roles that MANAGE infrastructure, platforms, and CI/CD systems at organizational scale. Multi-team or org-wide responsibility. Primary output is stable, scalable platforms.
definitions.Platform & Tooling: Roles that build INTERNAL developer platforms, observability tools, API gateways, auth systems, internal SDKs. Output is tooling consumed by other engineers.
definitions.Product & Design: Roles focused on product strategy, UX design, user research, or product management. Primary output is product roadmaps, design artifacts, or user research insights.

disambiguation_rules: App Engineering vs Infrastructure: If the role's PRIMARY output is running software used by end users → App Engineering. If managing shared platforms for other teams → Infrastructure.
App Engineering vs Data: If building ML-powered features that run in an app → App Engineering. If building the data platform that trains models → Data & Intelligence.
Platform & Tooling vs Infrastructure: Platform engineering builds tools for developers (SDKs, APIs). Infrastructure manages compute/network/storage at org scale.
Cyber Security vs App Engineering: Application Security Engineers who write security features belong in App Engineering. Dedicated security teams (SOC, pen test) belong in Cyber Security.
Product & Design vs App Engineering: A TPM or Product Manager is Product & Design. An engineer who also does product planning is still App Engineering.
